tldr: 本文研究了预训练对卷积神经网络剪枝效率的影响，比较了微调和从零训练两种策略的剪枝效果。
motivation: 深度神经网络在移动端和实时应用中面临计算与内存资源限制，剪枝可减少冗余参数，但训练策略对剪枝效率的影响尚不明确。
method: 在CIFAR10、CIFAR100等四个数据集和VGG16、MobileNet两种网络上，对比了预训练微调与从零训练的剪枝效果。
result: 预训练微调网络可实现高达80%的参数剪枝，且性能损失较小，优于从零训练的剪枝效果。
conclusion: 预训练微调显著提升CNN剪枝效率，为资源受限场景下的模型部署提供了有效方法。
search_text: An Experimental Study of the Impact of Pre-training on the Pruning of a Convolutional Neural Network 预训练对卷积神经网络剪枝影响的实验研究 In recent years, deep neural networks have known a wide success in various application domains. However, they require important computational and memory resources, which severely hinders their deployment, notably on mobile devices or for real-time applications. Neural networks usually involve a large number of parameters, which correspond to the weights of the network. Such parameters, obtained with the help of a training process, are determinant for the performance of the network. However, they are also highly redundant. The pruning methods notably attempt to reduce the size of the parameter set, by identifying and removing the irrelevant weights. In this paper, we examine the impact of the training strategy on the pruning efficiency. Two training modalities are considered and compared: (1) fine-tuned and (2) from scratch. The experimental results obtained on four datasets (CIFAR10, CIFAR100, SVHN and Caltech101) and for two different CNNs (VGG16 and MobileNet) demonstrate that a network that has been pre-trained on a large corpus (e.g. ImageNet) and then fine-tuned on a particular dataset can be pruned much more efficiently (up to 80% of parameter reduction) than the same network trained from scratch. 近年来，深度神经网络在各个应用领域取得了广泛的成功。然而，它们需要重要的计算和内存资源，这严重阻碍了它们的部署，特别是在移动设备或实时应用程序上。神经网络通常涉及大量参数，这些参数对应于网络的权重。在训练过程的帮助下获得的这些参数决定了网络的性能。然而，它们也是高度冗余的。剪枝方法特别试图通过识别和删除不相关的权重来减小参数集的大小。在本文中，我们研究了训练策略对剪枝效率的影响。考虑并比较了两种培训方式：（1）微调和（2）从头开始。在四个数据集（CIFAR10、CIFAR100、SVHN 和 Caltech101）和两个不同 CNN（VGG16 和 MobileNet）上获得的实验结果表明，与从头开始训练的相同网络相比，在大型语料库（例如 ImageNet）上进行预训练然后在特定数据集上进行微调的网络可以更有效地进行修剪（参数减少高达 80%）。 本文研究了预训练对卷积神经网络剪枝效率的影响，比较了微调和从零训练两种策略的剪枝效果。 Nathan Hubens,Matei Mancas,Bernard Gosselin,Marius Preda,Titus Zaharia cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: María Jesús Cano-Martínez,Miguel Carrasco,Joaquín Sandoval,César González-Martín
title: Quantitative analysis of visual representation of sign elements in COVID-19 context
title_zh: COVID-19背景下符号元素视觉表示的定量分析
abstract: Representation is the way in which human beings re-present the reality of what is happening, both externally and internally. Thus, visual representation as a means of communication uses elements to build a narrative, just as spoken and written language do. We propose using computer analysis to perform a quantitative analysis of the elements used in the visual creations that have been produced in reference to the epidemic, using the images compiled in The Covid Art Museum's Instagram account to analyze the different elements used to represent subjective experiences with regard to a global event. This process has been carried out with techniques based on machine learning to detect objects in the images so that the algorithm can be capable of learning and detecting the objects contained in each study image. This research reveals that the elements that are repeated in images to create narratives and the relations of association that are established in the sample, concluding that, despite the subjectivity that all creation entails, there are certain parameters of shared and reduced decisions when it comes to selecting objects to be included in visual representations
summary: Representation is the way in which human beings re-present the reality of what is happening, both externally and internally. abstract_zh: 表征是人类再现外部和内部正在发生的现实的方式。因此，视觉表现作为一种交流手段，使用元素来构建叙事，就像口语和书面语言一样。我们建议使用计算机分析对针对疫情而制作的视觉创作中使用的元素进行定量分析，使用 Covid 艺术博物馆 Instagram 帐户中编译的图像来分析用于代表全球事件主观体验的不同元素。该过程是通过基于机器学习的技术来执行的，以检测图像中的对象，以便算法能够学习和检测每个研究图像中包含的对象。这项研究揭示了图像中重复创建叙事的元素以及样本中建立的关联关系，得出的结论是，尽管所有创作都具有主观性，但在选择要包含在视觉表示中的对象时，存在某些共享和简化决策的参数

AI.tldr: 本文通过计算机分析方法，对新冠疫情期间视觉创作中的符号元素进行量化研究，探讨其叙事方式。
AI.motivation: 视觉表征是人类传递现实经验的重要方式，研究其在疫情背景下的元素使用规律有助于理解集体认知与主观体验的关联。
AI.method: 基于机器学习的图像物体检测技术，分析《新冠艺术博物馆》Instagram账号中的图像数据，量化提取重复出现的视觉元素及关联关系。
AI.result: 发现尽管创作具有主观性，但样本中存在共性元素选择模式，如口罩、手部消毒等符号被高频使用以构建疫情叙事。
AI.conclusion: 视觉表征在主观创作中仍存在共享的符号选择逻辑，量化分析可揭示集体事件中人类认知的共性特征。

authors_text: D. Aloini,A. Fronzetti Colladon,P. Gloor,E. Guerrazzi,A. Stefanini
title: Enhancing operations management through smart sensors: measuring and improving well-being, interaction and performance of logistics workers
title_zh: 通过智能传感器加强运营管理：衡量和改善物流工人的福祉、互动和绩效
abstract: Purpose The purpose of the research is to conduct an exploratory investigation of the material handling activities of an Italian logistics hub. Wearable sensors and other smart tools were used for collecting human and environmental features during working activities. These factors were correlated with workers' performance and well-being. Design/methodology/approach Human and environmental factors play an important role in operations management activities since they significantly influence employees' performance, well-being and safety. Surprisingly, empirical studies about the impact of such aspects on logistics operations are still very limited. Trying to fill this gap, the research empirically explores human and environmental factors affecting the performance of logistics workers exploiting smart tools. Findings Results suggest that human attitudes, interactions, emotions and environmental conditions remarkably influence workers' performance and well-being, however, showing different relationships depending on individual characteristics of each worker. Practical implications The authors' research opens up new avenues for profiling employees and adopting an individualized human resource management, providing managers with an operational system capable to potentially check and improve workers' well-being and performance. Originality/value The originality of the study comes from the in-depth exploration of human and environmental factors using body-worn sensors during work activities, by recording individual, collaborative and environmental data in real-time. To the best of the authors' knowledge, the current paper is the first time that such a detailed analysis has been carried out in real-world logistics operations.
summary: Purpose The purpose of the research is to conduct an exploratory investigation of the material handling activities of an Italian logistics hub. Wearable sensors and other smart tools were used for collecting human and environmental features during working activities. These factors were correlated with workers' performance and well-being. Originality/value The originality of the study comes from the in-depth exploration of human and environmental factors using body-worn sensors during work activities, by recording individual, collaborative and environmental data in real-time. To the best of the authors' knowledge, the current paper is the first time that such a detailed analysis has been carried out in real-world logistics operations.
abstract_zh: 目的 本研究的目的是对意大利物流中心的物料搬运活动进行探索性调查。可穿戴传感器和其他智能工具用于收集工作活动期间的人体和环境特征。这些因素与工人的绩效和福祉相关。设计/方法论/途径 人类和环境因素在运营管理活动中发挥着重要作用，因为它们显着影响员工的绩效、福祉和安全。令人惊讶的是，关于这些方面对物流运营影响的实证研究仍然非常有限。为了填补这一空白，该研究实证探索了影响物流工人利用智能工具绩效的人类和环境因素。结果表明，人类的态度、互动、情绪和环境条件显着影响工人的绩效和幸福感，然而，根据每个工人的个人特征，表现出不同的关系。实际意义 作者的研究为分析员工和采用个性化人力资源管理开辟了新途径，为管理者提供了一个能够检查和改善员工福祉和绩效的操作系统。原创性/价值该研究的原创性来自于在工作活动中使用穿戴式传感器，通过实时记录个人、协作和环境数据来深入探索人类和环境因素。据作者所知，本文是首次在现实世界的物流运营中进行如此详细的分析。

AI.tldr: 通过智能传感器提升物流工人福祉、互动与绩效的运营管理研究
AI.motivation: 现有研究缺乏对物流作业中人类与环境因素影响的实证分析，需探索其对工人绩效和福祉的具体作用机制
AI.method: 采用可穿戴传感器等智能工具实时采集物流工人个体、协作及环境数据，建立多维因素与绩效/福祉的关联模型
AI.result: 发现人类态度、互动、情绪及环境条件显著影响工人绩效与福祉，但不同个体呈现差异化关联特征
AI.conclusion: 首次在真实物流场景中通过体感传感器实现多维度数据采集，为个性化人力资源管理提供操作性系统支持

authors_text: Christopher Yacoumatos,Stefano Bragaglia,Anshul Kanakia,Nils Svangård,Jonathan Mangion,Claire Donoghue,Jim Weatherall,Faisal M. Khan,Khader Shameer
title: TrialGraph: Machine Intelligence Enabled Insight from Graph Modelling of Clinical Trials
title_zh: TrialGraph：机器智能从临床试验的图形建模中获得洞察力
abstract: A major impediment to successful drug development is the complexity, cost, and scale of clinical trials. The detailed internal structure of clinical trial data can make conventional optimization difficult to achieve. Recent advances in machine learning, specifically graph-structured data analysis, have the potential to enable significant progress in improving the clinical trial design. TrialGraph seeks to apply these methodologies to produce a proof-of-concept framework for developing models which can aid drug development and benefit patients. In this work, we first introduce a curated clinical trial data set compiled from the CT.gov, AACT and TrialTrove databases (n=1191 trials; representing one million patients) and describe the conversion of this data to graph-structured formats. We then detail the mathematical basis and implementation of a selection of graph machine learning algorithms, which typically use standard machine classifiers on graph data embedded in a low-dimensional feature space. We trained these models to predict side effect information for a clinical trial given information on the disease, existing medical conditions, and treatment. The MetaPath2Vec algorithm performed exceptionally well, with standard Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Support Vector, and Neural Network classifiers exhibiting typical ROC-AUC scores of 0.85, 0.68, 0.86, 0.80, and 0.77, respectively. Remarkably, the best performing classifiers could only produce typical ROC-AUC scores of 0.70 when trained on equivalent array-structured data. Our work demonstrates that graph modelling can significantly improve prediction accuracy on appropriate datasets. Successive versions of the project that refine modelling assumptions and incorporate more data types can produce excellent predictors with real-world applications in drug development.

The detailed internal structure of clinical trial data can make conventional optimization difficult to achieve. Recent advances in machine learning, specifically graph-structured data analysis, have the potential to enable significant progress in improving the clinical trial design. TrialGraph seeks to apply these methodologies to produce a proof-of-concept framework for developing models which can aid drug development and benefit patients. In this work, we first introduce a curated clinical trial data set compiled from the CT.gov, AACT and TrialTrove databases (n=1191 trials; representing one million patients) and describe the conversion of this data to graph-structured formats. We then detail the mathematical basis and implementation of a selection of graph machine learning algorithms, which typically use standard machine classifiers on graph data embedded in a low-dimensional feature space. We trained these models to predict side effect information for a clinical trial given information on the disease, existing medical conditions, and treatment. The MetaPath2Vec algorithm performed exceptionally well, with standard Logistic Regression, Decision Tree, Random Forest, Support Vector, and Neural Network classifiers exhibiting typical ROC-AUC scores of 0.85, 0.68, 0.86, 0.80, and 0.77, respectively. Remarkably, the best performing classifiers could only produce typical ROC-AUC scores of 0.70 when trained on equivalent array-structured data. Our work demonstrates that graph modelling can significantly improve prediction accuracy on appropriate datasets. Successive versions of the project that refine modelling assumptions and incorporate more data types can produce excellent predictors with real-world applications in drug development.
abstract_zh: 药物开发成功的主要障碍是临床试验的复杂性、成本和规模。临床试验数据的详细内部结构使得常规优化难以实现。机器学习（特别是图形结构数据分析）的最新进展有可能在改进临床试验设计方面取得重大进展。 TrialGraph 试图应用这些方法来生成一个概念验证框架，用于开发可以帮助药物开发并造福患者的模型。在这项工作中，我们首先介绍了从 CT.gov、AACT 和 TrialTrove 数据库（n=1191 项试验；代表 100 万患者）编译的精选临床试验数据集，并描述了该数据到图形结构格式的转换。然后，我们详细介绍了一系列图机器学习算法的数学基础和实现，这些算法通常对嵌入低维特征空间的图数据使用标准机器分类器。我们训练这些模型，根据有关疾病、现有医疗状况和治疗的信息来预测临床试验的副作用信息。 MetaPath2Vec 算法表现非常出色，标准逻辑回归、决策树、随机森林、支持向量和神经网络分类器的典型 ROC-AUC 分数分别为 0.85、0.68、0.86、0.80 和 0.77。值得注意的是，当使用等效的数组结构数据进行训练时，性能最佳的分类器只能产生 0.70 的典型 ROC-AUC 分数。我们的工作表明，图建模可以显着提高适当数据集的预测准确性。该项目的后续版本改进了建模假设并纳入了更多数据类型，可以产生出色的预测器，并在药物开发的实际应用中得到应用。

AI.tldr: 开发基于图模型和机器学习的框架，以改进临床试验设计并预测药物副作用，从而加速药物开发。
AI.motivation: 传统方法难以处理临床试验数据的复杂性和规模，需更高效模型提升预测准确性并优化试验设计。
AI.method: 构建包含1191项临床试验的图结构数据集，应用MetaPath2Vec等算法结合多种分类器（逻辑回归、随机森林等）预测副作用。
AI.result: 图模型显著提升预测性能，最佳分类器ROC-AUC达0.86，而传统数组结构数据仅达0.70。
AI.conclusion: 图建模能有效提升临床试验预测准确性，未来优化模型和数据类型可实现实际药物开发应用。

authors_text: Igor Janos,Wanda Benesova
title: Single Image Automatic Radial Distortion Compensation Using Deep Convolutional Network
title_zh: 使用深度卷积网络的单图像自动径向畸变补偿
abstract: In many computer vision domains, the input images must conform with the pinhole camera model, where straight lines in the real world are projected as straight lines in the image. Performing computer vision tasks on live sports broadcast footage imposes challenging requirements where the algorithms cannot rely on a specific calibration pattern must be able to cope with unknown and uncalibrated cameras, radial distortion originating from complex television lenses, few visual clues to compensate distortion by, and the necessity for real-time performance. We present a novel method for single-image automatic lens distortion compensation based on deep convolutional neural networks, capable of real-time performance and accuracy using two highest-order coefficients of the polynomial distortion model operating in the application domain of sports broadcast. Keywords: Deep Convolutional Neural Network, Radial Distortion, Single Image Rectification
summary: In many computer vision domains, the input images must conform with the pinhole camera model, where straight lines in the real world are projected as straight lines in the image. Performing computer vision tasks on live sports broadcast footage imposes challenging requirements where the algorithms cannot rely on a specific calibration pattern must be able to cope with unknown and uncalibrated cameras, radial distortion originating from complex television lenses, few visual clues to compensate distortion by, and the necessity for real-time performance. We present a novel method for single-image automatic lens distortion compensation based on deep convolutional neural networks, capable of real-time performance and accuracy using two highest-order coefficients of the polynomial distortion model operating in the application domain of sports broadcast. Keywords: Deep Convolutional Neural Network, Radial Distortion, Single Image Rectification
abstract_zh: 在许多计算机视觉领域中，输入图像必须符合针孔相机模型，其中现实世界中的直线在图像中投影为直线。在体育直播镜头上执行计算机视觉任务提出了具有挑战性的要求，其中算法不能依赖于特定的校准模式，必须能够应对未知和未校准的摄像机、源自复杂电视镜头的径向失真、补偿失真的视觉线索很少以及实时性能的必要性。我们提出了一种基于深度卷积神经网络的单图像自动镜头畸变补偿的新方法，能够使用在体育广播应用领域中运行的多项式畸变模型的两个最高阶系数来实现实时性能和准确性。关键词：深度卷积神经网络，径向畸变，单幅图像校正

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度卷积神经网络的单图像自动径向畸变校正方法，适用于体育赛事直播等实时场景。
AI.motivation: 传统计算机视觉任务需依赖标定相机模型，但体育直播等场景中相机参数未知且存在复杂镜头畸变，缺乏实时校正方法。
AI.method: 设计深度卷积网络直接估计多项式畸变模型的高阶系数，通过端到端学习实现单图像实时畸变校正。
AI.result: 方法在体育直播场景中实现高精度实时校正，无需标定图案且能处理未知镜头畸变。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为无标定实时图像校正提供了有效解决方案，可扩展至其他复杂畸变场景。

authors_text: Furkan Luleci,F. Necati Catbas,Onur Avci
title: Generative Adversarial Networks for Data Generation in Structural Health Monitoring
title_zh: 用于结构健康监测中数据生成的生成对抗网络
abstract: Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) has been continuously benefiting from the advancements in the field of data science. Various types of Artificial Intelligence (AI) methods have been utilized for the assessment and evaluation of civil structures. In AI, Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) algorithms require plenty of datasets to train; particularly, the more data DL models are trained with, the better output it yields. Yet, in SHM applications, collecting data from civil structures through sensors is expensive and obtaining useful data (damage associated data) is challenging. In this paper, 1-D Wasserstein loss Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Networks using Gradient Penalty (1-D WDCGAN-GP) is utilized to generate damage associated vibration datasets that are similar to the input. For the purpose of vibration-based damage diagnostics, a 1-D Deep Convolutional Neural Network (1-D DCNN) is built, trained, and tested on both real and generated datasets. The classification results from the 1-D DCNN on both datasets resulted to be very similar to each other. The presented work in this paper shows that for the cases of insufficient data in DL or ML-based damage diagnostics, 1-D WDCGAN-GP can successfully generate data for the model to be trained on. Keywords: 1-D Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN), Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Networks (DCGAN), Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Networks with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP), 1-D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Structural Health Monitoring (SHM), Structural Damage Diagnostics, Structural Damage Detection
summary: Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) has been continuously benefiting from the advancements in the field of data science. Various types of Artificial Intelligence (AI) methods have been utilized for the assessment and evaluation of civil structures. In AI, Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) algorithms require plenty of datasets to train; particularly, the more data DL models are trained with, the better output it yields. Yet, in SHM applications, collecting data from civil structures through sensors is expensive and obtaining useful data (damage associated data) is challenging. In this paper, 1-D Wasserstein loss Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Networks using Gradient Penalty (1-D WDCGAN-GP) is utilized to generate damage associated vibration datasets that are similar to the input. For the purpose of vibration-based damage diagnostics, a 1-D Deep Convolutional Neural Network (1-D DCNN) is built, trained, and tested on both real and generated datasets. The classification results from the 1-D DCNN on both datasets resulted to be very similar to each other. The presented work in this paper shows that for the cases of insufficient data in DL or ML-based damage diagnostics, 1-D WDCGAN-GP can successfully generate data for the model to be trained on. Keywords: 1-D Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN), Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Networks (DCGAN), Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Networks with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP), 1-D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Structural Health Monitoring (SHM), Structural Damage Diagnostics, Structural Damage Detection
abstract_zh: 结构健康监测（SHM）不断受益于数据科学领域的进步。各种类型的人工智能（AI）方法已用于土木结构的评估和评估。在人工智能领域，机器学习（ML）和深度学习（DL）算法需要大量数据集来训练；特别是，深度学习模型训练的数据越多，产生的输出就越好。然而，在健康监测应用中，通过传感器从土木结构收​​集数据的成本很高，并且获取有用的数据（损坏相关数据）也具有挑战性。在本文中，利用梯度惩罚的一维 Wasserstein 损失深度卷积生成对抗网络（一维 WDCGAN-GP）来生成与输入相似的损伤相关振动数据集。为了进行基于振动的损伤诊断，我们在真实数据集和生成数据集上构建、训练和测试了一维深度卷积神经网络 (1-D DCNN)。两个数据集上的一维 DCNN 的分类结果非常相似。本文提出的工作表明，对于基于深度学习或机器学习的损伤诊断数据不足的情况，一维 WDCGAN-GP 可以成功生成用于训练模型的数据。关键词：一维生成对抗网络（GAN）、深度卷积生成对抗网络（DCGAN）、带梯度惩罚的 Wasserstein 生成对抗网络（WGAN-GP）、一维卷积神经网络（CNN）、结构健康监测（SHM）、结构损伤诊断、结构损伤检测

AI.tldr: 本文提出使用1-D WDCGAN-GP生成结构健康监测中的损伤相关振动数据，以解决实际数据不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 结构健康监测中实际损伤数据获取成本高且困难，导致机器学习模型训练数据不足，影响诊断效果。
AI.method: 采用带梯度惩罚的1-D Wasserstein深度卷积生成对抗网络（1-D WDCGAN-GP）生成振动数据，并构建1-D卷积神经网络（DCNN）在真实与生成数据集上进行训练与测试。
AI.result: DCNN在真实数据集和生成数据集上的分类结果高度相似，表明生成数据能有效支持模型训练。
AI.conclusion: 当数据不足时，1-D WDCGAN-GP可成功生成高质量数据，为基于深度学习的结构损伤诊断提供可行方案。

search_text: Generative Adversarial Networks for Data Generation in Structural Health Monitoring 用于结构健康监测中数据生成的生成对抗网络 Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) has been continuously benefiting from the advancements in the field of data science. Various types of Artificial Intelligence (AI) methods have been utilized for the assessment and evaluation of civil structures. In AI, Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) algorithms require plenty of datasets to train; particularly, the more data DL models are trained with, the better output it yields. Yet, in SHM applications, collecting data from civil structures through sensors is expensive and obtaining useful data (damage associated data) is challenging. In this paper, 1-D Wasserstein loss Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Networks using Gradient Penalty (1-D WDCGAN-GP) is utilized to generate damage associated vibration datasets that are similar to the input. For the purpose of vibration-based damage diagnostics, a 1-D Deep Convolutional Neural Network (1-D DCNN) is built, trained, and tested on both real and generated datasets. The classification results from the 1-D DCNN on both datasets resulted to be very similar to each other. The presented work in this paper shows that for the cases of insufficient data in DL or ML-based damage diagnostics, 1-D WDCGAN-GP can successfully generate data for the model to be trained on. Keywords: 1-D Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN), Deep Convolutional Generative Adversarial Networks (DCGAN), Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Networks with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP), 1-D Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN), Structural Health Monitoring (SHM), Structural Damage Diagnostics, Structural Damage Detection 结构健康监测（SHM）不断受益于数据科学领域的进步。各种类型的人工智能（AI）方法已用于土木结构的评估和评估。在人工智能领域，机器学习（ML）和深度学习（DL）算法需要大量数据集来训练；特别是，深度学习模型训练的数据越多，产生的输出就越好。然而，在健康监测应用中，通过传感器从土木结构收​​集数据的成本很高，并且获取有用的数据（损坏相关数据）也具有挑战性。在本文中，利用梯度惩罚的一维 Wasserstein 损失深度卷积生成对抗网络（一维 WDCGAN-GP）来生成与输入相似的损伤相关振动数据集。为了进行基于振动的损伤诊断，我们在真实数据集和生成数据集上构建、训练和测试了一维深度卷积神经网络 (1-D DCNN)。两个数据集上的一维 DCNN 的分类结果非常相似。本文提出的工作表明，对于基于深度学习或机器学习的损伤诊断数据不足的情况，一维 WDCGAN-GP 可以成功生成用于训练模型的数据。关键词：一维生成对抗网络（GAN）、深度卷积生成对抗网络（DCGAN）、带梯度惩罚的 Wasserstein 生成对抗网络（WGAN-GP）、一维卷积神经网络（CNN）、结构健康监测（SHM）、结构损伤诊断、结构损伤检测 本文提出使用1-D WDCGAN-GP生成结构健康监测中的损伤相关振动数据，以解决实际数据不足的问题。 Furkan Luleci,F. authors_text: Furkan Luleci,F. Necati Catbas,Onur Avci
title: Generative Adversarial Networks for Labelled Vibration Data Generation
title_zh: 用于生成标记振动数据的生成对抗网络
abstract: As Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) being implemented more over the years, the use of operational modal analysis of civil structures has become more significant for the assessment and evaluation of engineering structures. Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) algorithms have been in use for structural damage diagnostics of civil structures in the last couple of decades. While collecting vibration data from civil structures is a challenging and expensive task for both undamaged and damaged cases, in this paper, the authors are introducing Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) that is built on the Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) and using Wasserstein Distance for generating artificial labelled data to be used for structural damage diagnostic purposes. The authors named the developed model 1D W-DCGAN and successfully generated vibration data which is very similar to the input. The methodology presented in this paper will pave the way for vibration data generation for numerous future applications in the SHM domain.
summary: As Structural Health Monitoring (SHM) being implemented more over the years, the use of operational modal analysis of civil structures has become more significant for the assessment and evaluation of engineering structures. Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) algorithms have been in use for structural damage diagnostics of civil structures in the last couple of decades. While collecting vibration data from civil structures is a challenging and expensive task for both undamaged and damaged cases, in this paper, the authors are introducing Generative Adversarial Networks (GAN) that is built on the Deep Convolutional Neural Network (DCNN) and using Wasserstein Distance for generating artificial labelled data to be used for structural damage diagnostic purposes. The authors named the developed model 1D W-DCGAN and successfully generated vibration data which is very similar to the input. The methodology presented in this paper will pave the way for vibration data generation for numerous future applications in the SHM domain.
abstract_zh: 随着结构健康监测（SHM）多年来的广泛应用，土木结构运行模态分析的使用对于工程结构的评估和评价变得更加重要。过去几十年来，机器学习 (ML) 和深度学习 (DL) 算法已用于土木结构的结构损伤诊断。虽然从土木结构中收集振动数据对于未损坏和损坏的情况来说都是一项具有挑战性且昂贵的任务，但在本文中，作者介绍了基于深度卷积神经网络 (DCNN) 构建的生成对抗网络 (GAN)，并使用 Wasserstein Distance 生成用于结构损坏诊断目的的人工标记数据。作者将开发的模型命名为 1D W-DCGAN，并成功生成了与输入非常相似的振动数据。本文提出的方法将为 SHM 领域众多未来应用的振动数据生成铺平道路。

AI.tldr: 提出基于Wasserstein距离的1D W-DCGAN模型，生成用于结构损伤诊断的标记振动数据
AI.motivation: 实际振动数据收集困难且成本高，需生成合成数据以支持结构损伤诊断模型的训练与应用
AI.method: 基于深度卷积神经网络（DCNN）的生成对抗网络（GAN），结合Wasserstein距离生成人工标记振动数据
AI.result: 成功生成与真实数据高度相似的振动数据，验证了模型有效性
AI.conclusion: 该方法为结构健康监测领域提供了高效生成振动数据的解决方案，促进未来应用

authors_text: Yu Gong,Zhihan Xu,Zhezhi He,Weifeng Zhang,Xiaobing Tu,Xiaoyao Liang,Li Jiang
title: N3H-Core: Neuron-designed Neural Network Accelerator via FPGA-based Heterogeneous Computing Cores
title_zh: N3H-Core：Neuron 设计的神经网络加速器，通过基于 FPGA 的异构计算内核
comment: 11 pages, 12 figures, In Proceedings of the 2022 ACM/SIGDA International Symposium on Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGA'22), February 27-March 1, 2022, Virtual Event, CA, USA
comments: 11 pages, 12 figures, In Proceedings of the 2022 ACM/SIGDA International Symposium on Field-Programmable Gate Arrays (FPGA'22), February 27-March 1, 2022, Virtual Event, CA, USA
abstract: Accelerating the neural network inference by FPGA has emerged as a popular option, since the reconfigurability and high performance computing capability of FPGA intrinsically satisfies the computation demand of the fast-evolving neural algorithms. However, the popular neural accelerators on FPGA (e.g., Xilinx DPU) mainly utilize the DSP resources for constructing their processing units, while the rich LUT resources are not well exploited. Via the software-hardware co-design approach, in this work, we develop an FPGA-based heterogeneous computing system for neural network acceleration. From the hardware perspective, the proposed accelerator consists of DSP- and LUT-based GEneral Matrix-Multiplication (GEMM) computing cores, which forms the entire computing system in a heterogeneous fashion. The DSP- and LUT-based GEMM cores are computed w.r.t a unified Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) and unified buffers. Along the data flow of the neural network inference path, the computation of the convolution/fully-connected layer is split into two portions, handled by the DSP- and LUT-based GEMM cores asynchronously. From the software perspective, we mathematically and systematically model the latency and resource utilization of the proposed heterogeneous accelerator, regarding varying system design configurations. Through leveraging the reinforcement learning technique, we construct a framework to achieve end-to-end selection and optimization of the design specification of target heterogeneous accelerator, including workload split strategy, mixed-precision quantization scheme, and resource allocation of DSP- and LUT-core. In virtue of the proposed design framework and heterogeneous computing system, our design outperforms the state-of-the-art Mix&Match design with latency reduced by 1.12-1.32x with higher inference accuracy. The N3H-core is open-sourced at: https://github.com/elliothe/N3H_Core.
summary: Accelerating the neural network inference by FPGA has emerged as a popular option, since the reconfigurability and high performance computing capability of FPGA intrinsically satisfies the computation demand of the fast-evolving neural algorithms. However, the popular neural accelerators on FPGA (e.g., Xilinx DPU) mainly utilize the DSP resources for constructing their processing units, while the rich LUT resources are not well exploited. Via the software-hardware co-design approach, in this work, we develop an FPGA-based heterogeneous computing system for neural network acceleration. From the hardware perspective, the proposed accelerator consists of DSP- and LUT-based GEneral Matrix-Multiplication (GEMM) computing cores, which forms the entire computing system in a heterogeneous fashion. The DSP- and LUT-based GEMM cores are computed w.r.t a unified Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) and unified buffers. Along the data flow of the neural network inference path, the computation of the convolution/fully-connected layer is split into two portions, handled by the DSP- and LUT-based GEMM cores asynchronously. From the software perspective, we mathematically and systematically model the latency and resource utilization of the proposed heterogeneous accelerator, regarding varying system design configurations. Through leveraging the reinforcement learning technique, we construct a framework to achieve end-to-end selection and optimization of the design specification of target heterogeneous accelerator, including workload split strategy, mixed-precision quantization scheme, and resource allocation of DSP- and LUT-core. In virtue of the proposed design framework and heterogeneous computing system, our design outperforms the state-of-the-art Mix&Match design with latency reduced by 1.12-1.32x with higher inference accuracy. The N3H-core is open-sourced at: https://github.com/elliothe/N3H_Core.
abstract_zh: 通过FPGA加速神经网络推理已成为一种流行的选择，因为FPGA的可重构性和高性能计算能力本质上满足了快速发展的神经算法的计算需求。然而，流行的FPGA上的神经加速器（例如Xilinx DPU）主要利用DSP资源来构建其处理单元，而丰富的LUT资源没有得到很好的利用。通过软硬件协同设计方法，在这项工作中，我们开发了一种基于 FPGA 的神经网络加速异构计算系统。从硬件角度来看，所提出的加速器由基于 DSP 和 LUT 的通用矩阵乘法 (GEMM) 计算核心组成，以异构方式形成整个计算系统。基于 DSP 和 LUT 的 GEMM 内核通过统一指令集架构 (ISA) 和统一缓冲区进行计算。沿着神经网络推理路径的数据流，卷积/全连接层的计算分为两部分，由基于 DSP 和 LUT 的 GEMM 内核异步处理。从软件角度来看，我们针对不同的系统设计配置，对所提出的异构加速器的延迟和资源利用率进行了数学和系统建模。通过利用强化学习技术，我们构建了一个框架来实现目标异构加速器设计规范的端到端选择和优化，包括工作负载分割策略、混合精度量化方案以及DSP和LUT核的资源分配。凭借所提出的设计框架和异构计算系统，我们的设计优于最先进的 Mix&Match 设计，延迟降低了 1.12-1.32 倍，推理精度更高。 N3H 核心已开源：https://github.com/elliothe/N3H_Core。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于FPGA的异构计算系统N3H-Core，用于加速神经网络推理。
AI.motivation: 尽管FPGA在神经网络加速方面具有可重构性和高性能计算能力，但现有加速器（如Xilinx DPU）主要利用DSP资源，未能充分利用LUT资源。
AI.method: 通过软硬件协同设计方法，开发了一个基于FPGA的异构计算系统，包含DSP和LUT为基础的GEMM计算核心，并使用强化学习技术进行设计规范的端到端选择和优化。
AI.result: 与最先进的Mix&Match设计相比，该设计在延迟上减少了1.12-1.32倍，同时保持更高的推理准确性。
AI.conclusion: N3H-Core通过充分利用FPGA的DSP和LUT资源，有效提升了神经网络推理的性能，并且已开源。

ai_content.motivation: 尽管FPGA在神经网络加速方面具有可重构性和高性能计算能力，但现有加速器（如Xilinx DPU）主要利用DSP资源，未能充分利用LUT资源。

ai_content.result: 与最先进的Mix&Match设计相比，该设计在延迟上减少了1.12-1.32倍，同时保持更高的推理准确性。
ai_content.conclusion: N3H-Core通过充分利用FPGA的DSP和LUT资源，有效提升了神经网络推理的性能，并且已开源。

search_text: N3H-Core: Neuron-designed Neural Network Accelerator via FPGA-based Heterogeneous Computing Cores N3H-Core：Neuron 设计的神经网络加速器，通过基于 FPGA 的异构计算内核 Accelerating the neural network inference by FPGA has emerged as a popular option, since the reconfigurability and high performance computing capability of FPGA intrinsically satisfies the computation demand of the fast-evolving neural algorithms. However, the popular neural accelerators on FPGA (e.g., Xilinx DPU) mainly utilize the DSP resources for constructing their processing units, while the rich LUT resources are not well exploited. Via the software-hardware co-design approach, in this work, we develop an FPGA-based heterogeneous computing system for neural network acceleration. From the hardware perspective, the proposed accelerator consists of DSP- and LUT-based GEneral Matrix-Multiplication (GEMM) computing cores, which forms the entire computing system in a heterogeneous fashion. The DSP- and LUT-based GEMM cores are computed w.r.t a unified Instruction Set Architecture (ISA) and unified buffers. Along the data flow of the neural network inference path, the computation of the convolution/fully-connected layer is split into two portions, handled by the DSP- and LUT-based GEMM cores asynchronously. From the software perspective, we mathematically and systematically model the latency and resource utilization of the proposed heterogeneous accelerator, regarding varying system design configurations. Through leveraging the reinforcement learning technique, we construct a framework to achieve end-to-end selection and optimization of the design specification of target heterogeneous accelerator, including workload split strategy, mixed-precision quantization scheme, and resource allocation of DSP- and LUT-core. In virtue of the proposed design framework and heterogeneous computing system, our design outperforms the state-of-the-art Mix&Match design with latency reduced by 1.12-1.32x with higher inference accuracy. The N3H-core is open-sourced at: https://github.com/elliothe/N3H_Core. 通过FPGA加速神经网络推理已成为一种流行的选择，因为FPGA的可重构性和高性能计算能力本质上满足了快速发展的神经算法的计算需求。然而，流行的FPGA上的神经加速器（例如Xilinx DPU）主要利用DSP资源来构建其处理单元，而丰富的LUT资源没有得到很好的利用。通过软硬件协同设计方法，在这项工作中，我们开发了一种基于 FPGA 的神经网络加速异构计算系统。从硬件角度来看，所提出的加速器由基于 DSP 和 LUT 的通用矩阵乘法 (GEMM) 计算核心组成，以异构方式形成整个计算系统。基于 DSP 和 LUT 的 GEMM 内核通过统一指令集架构 (ISA) 和统一缓冲区进行计算。沿着神经网络推理路径的数据流，卷积/全连接层的计算分为两部分，由基于 DSP 和 LUT 的 GEMM 内核异步处理。从软件角度来看，我们针对不同的系统设计配置，对所提出的异构加速器的延迟和资源利用率进行了数学和系统建模。通过利用强化学习技术，我们构建了一个框架来实现目标异构加速器设计规范的端到端选择和优化，包括工作负载分割策略、混合精度量化方案以及DSP和LUT核的资源分配。凭借所提出的设计框架和异构计算系统，我们的设计优于最先进的 Mix&Match 设计，延迟降低了 1.12-1.32 倍，推理精度更高。 N3H 核心已开源：https://github.com/elliothe/N3H_Core。 本文提出了一种基于FPGA的异构计算系统N3H-Core，用于加速神经网络推理。 Yu Gong,Zhihan Xu,Zhezhi He,Weifeng Zhang,Xiaobing Tu,Xiaoyao Liang,Li Jiang cs.AR,cs.AI,cs.PF

authors_text: Mobarakol Islam,Vibashan VS,Chwee Ming Lim,Hongliang Ren
title: ST-MTL: Spatio-Temporal Multitask Learning Model to Predict Scanpath While Tracking Instruments in Robotic Surgery
title_zh: ST-MTL：时空多任务学习模型，用于预测扫描路径，同时跟踪机器人手术中的仪器
abstract: Representation learning of the task-oriented attention while tracking instrument holds vast potential in image-guided robotic surgery. Incorporating cognitive ability to automate the camera control enables the surgeon to concentrate more on dealing with surgical instruments. The objective is to reduce the operation time and facilitate the surgery for both surgeons and patients. We propose an end-to-end trainable Spatio-Temporal Multi-Task Learning (ST-MTL) model with a shared encoder and spatio-temporal decoders for the real-time surgical instrument segmentation and task-oriented saliency detection. In the MTL model of shared parameters, optimizing multiple loss functions into a convergence point is still an open challenge. We tackle the problem with a novel asynchronous spatio-temporal optimization (ASTO) technique by calculating independent gradients for each decoder. We also design a competitive squeeze and excitation unit by casting a skip connection that retains weak features, excites strong features, and performs dynamic spatial and channel-wise feature recalibration. To capture better long term spatio-temporal dependencies, we enhance the long-short term memory (LSTM) module by concatenating high-level encoder features of consecutive frames. We also introduce Sinkhorn regularized loss to enhance task-oriented saliency detection by preserving computational efficiency. We generate the task-aware saliency maps and scanpath of the instruments on the dataset of the MICCAI 2017 robotic instrument segmentation challenge. Compared to the state-of-the-art segmentation and saliency methods, our model outperforms most of the evaluation metrics and produces an outstanding performance in the challenge.
summary: Representation learning of the task-oriented attention while tracking instrument holds vast potential in image-guided robotic surgery. Incorporating cognitive ability to automate the camera control enables the surgeon to concentrate more on dealing with surgical instruments. The objective is to reduce the operation time and facilitate the surgery for both surgeons and patients. We propose an end-to-end trainable Spatio-Temporal Multi-Task Learning (ST-MTL) model with a shared encoder and spatio-temporal decoders for the real-time surgical instrument segmentation and task-oriented saliency detection. In the MTL model of shared parameters, optimizing multiple loss functions into a convergence point is still an open challenge. We tackle the problem with a novel asynchronous spatio-temporal optimization (ASTO) technique by calculating independent gradients for each decoder. We also design a competitive squeeze and excitation unit by casting a skip connection that retains weak features, excites strong features, and performs dynamic spatial and channel-wise feature recalibration. To capture better long term spatio-temporal dependencies, we enhance the long-short term memory (LSTM) module by concatenating high-level encoder features of consecutive frames. We also introduce Sinkhorn regularized loss to enhance task-oriented saliency detection by preserving computational efficiency. We generate the task-aware saliency maps and scanpath of the instruments on the dataset of the MICCAI 2017 robotic instrument segmentation challenge. Compared to the state-of-the-art segmentation and saliency methods, our model outperforms most of the evaluation metrics and produces an outstanding performance in the challenge.
abstract_zh: 跟踪仪器中面向任务的注意力的表示学习在图像引导机器人手术中具有巨大的潜力。结合认知能力来实现摄像机控制的自动化，使外科医生能够更加专注于处理手术器械。目的是减少手术时间，方便外科医生和患者进行手术。我们提出了一种端到端可训练的时空多任务学习（ST-MTL）模型，具有共享编码器和时空解码器，用于实时手术器械分割和面向任务的显着性检测。在共享参数的 MTL 模型中，将多个损失函数优化为一个收敛点仍然是一个开放的挑战。我们通过计算每个解码器的独立梯度，使用新颖的异步时空优化（ASTO）技术来解决这个问题。我们还通过铸造跳跃连接来设计一个有竞争力的挤压和激励单元，该连接保留弱特征，激发强特征，并执行动态空间和通道特征重新校准。为了捕获更好的长期时空依赖性，我们通过连接连续帧的高级编码器特征来增强长短期记忆（LSTM）模块。我们还引入了 Sinkhorn 正则化损失，通过保持计算效率来增强面向任务的显着性检测。我们在 MICCAI 2017 机器人仪器分割挑战赛的数据集上生成仪器的任务感知显着性图和扫描路径。与最先进的分割和显着性方法相比，我们的模型优于大多数评估指标，并在挑战中表现出色。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种端到端的时空多任务学习模型（ST-MTL），用于实时手术器械分割和任务导向显著性检测，以预测机器人手术中的扫描路径。
AI.motivation: 通过自动化摄像机控制，增强手术中对器械的注意力建模，可减少操作时间并提升手术效率，同时减轻外科医生的认知负担。
AI.method: 设计共享编码器与时空解码器的ST-MTL框架，结合异步时空优化（ASTO）技术、竞争性压缩激励单元、增强LSTM模块及Sinkhorn正则化损失函数，实现多任务联合优化。
AI.result: 在MICCAI 2017数据集上，模型在分割和显著性检测任务中超越现有方法，取得挑战赛中的优异性能表现。
AI.conclusion: 提出的ST-MTL模型通过创新的多任务学习架构和优化技术，有效解决了手术场景下时空依赖建模与注意力预测的难题。

authors_text: Francesco d'Amore,Daniel Mitropolsky,Pierluigi Crescenzi,Emanuele Natale,Christos H. Papadimitriou
title: Planning with Biological Neurons and Synapses
title_zh: 使用生物神经元和突触进行规划
abstract: We revisit the planning problem in the blocks world, and we implement a known heuristic for this task. Importantly, our implementation is biologically plausible, in the sense that it is carried out exclusively through the spiking of neurons. Even though much has been accomplished in the blocks world over the past five decades, we believe that this is the first algorithm of its kind. The input is a sequence of symbols encoding an initial set of block stacks as well as a target set, and the output is a sequence of motion commands such as "put the top block in stack 1 on the table". The program is written in the Assembly Calculus, a recently proposed computational framework meant to model computation in the brain by bridging the gap between neural activity and cognitive function. Its elementary objects are assemblies of neurons (stable sets of neurons whose simultaneous firing signifies that the subject is thinking of an object, concept, word, etc.), its commands include project and merge, and its execution model is based on widely accepted tenets of neuroscience. A program in this framework essentially sets up a dynamical system of neurons and synapses that eventually, with high probability, accomplishes the task. The purpose of this work is to establish empirically that reasonably large programs in the Assembly Calculus can execute correctly and reliably; and that rather realistic -- if idealized -- higher cognitive functions, such as planning in the blocks world, can be implemented successfully by such programs.

Its elementary objects are assemblies of neurons (stable sets of neurons whose simultaneous firing signifies that the subject is thinking of an object, concept, word, etc.), its commands include project and merge, and its execution model is based on widely accepted tenets of neuroscience. A program in this framework essentially sets up a dynamical system of neurons and synapses that eventually, with high probability, accomplishes the task. The purpose of this work is to establish empirically that reasonably large programs in the Assembly Calculus can execute correctly and reliably; and that rather realistic -- if idealized -- higher cognitive functions, such as planning in the blocks world, can be implemented successfully by such programs.
abstract_zh: 我们重新审视区块世界中的规划问题，并为此任务实施一个已知的启发式方法。重要的是，我们的实现在生物学上是合理的，因为它完全是通过神经元的尖峰来实现的。尽管在过去的五年里区块世界已经取得了很大的成就，但我们相信这是第一个此类算法。输入是对初始块堆栈集以及目标集进行编码的符号序列，输出是运动命令序列，例如“将堆栈 1 中的顶部块放在桌子上”。该程序是用汇编微积分编写的，这是最近提出的一个计算框架，旨在通过弥合神经活动和认知功能之间的差距来模拟大脑中的计算。它的基本对象是神经元的集合（稳定的神经元组，其同时放电表示主体正在思考一个对象、概念、单词等），它的命令包括投射和合并，其执行模型基于广泛接受的神经科学原则。该框架中的程序本质上建立了一个由神经元和突触组成的动态系统，最终很有可能完成任务。这项工作的目的是根据经验确定汇编微积分中相当大的程序可以正确可靠地执行；而且相当现实的（如果理想化的话）更高的认知功能，例如在积木世界中进行规划，可以通过此类程序成功实现。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生物神经元和突触的积木世界规划算法，通过Assembly Calculus框架实现认知功能的生物可解释计算。
AI.motivation: 尽管积木世界研究已有五十年，但尚未有算法能以生物可解释方式实现规划功能，需验证神经科学原理驱动的计算框架可行性。
AI.method: 采用Assembly Calculus框架，以神经元集合（assembly）为基本单元，通过project/merge指令构建神经动态系统模拟认知过程。
AI.result: 实验证明大型Assembly Calculus程序可可靠执行，成功实现积木世界规划等理想化高级认知功能。
AI.conclusion: 生物神经元计算框架能有效实现复杂认知任务，为脑模拟与神经计算提供新方法论支持。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生物神经元和突触的积木世界规划算法，通过Assembly Calculus框架实现认知功能的生物可解释计算。

search_text: Planning with Biological Neurons and Synapses 使用生物神经元和突触进行规划 We revisit the planning problem in the blocks world, and we implement a known heuristic for this task. Importantly, our implementation is biologically plausible, in the sense that it is carried out exclusively through the spiking of neurons. Even though much has been accomplished in the blocks world over the past five decades, we believe that this is the first algorithm of its kind. The input is a sequence of symbols encoding an initial set of block stacks as well as a target set, and the output is a sequence of motion commands such as "put the top block in stack 1 on the table". The program is written in the Assembly Calculus, a recently proposed computational framework meant to model computation in the brain by bridging the gap between neural activity and cognitive function. Its elementary objects are assemblies of neurons (stable sets of neurons whose simultaneous firing signifies that the subject is thinking of an object, concept, word, etc.), its commands include project and merge, and its execution model is based on widely accepted tenets of neuroscience. A program in this framework essentially sets up a dynamical system of neurons and synapses that eventually, with high probability, accomplishes the task. The purpose of this work is to establish empirically that reasonably large programs in the Assembly Calculus can execute correctly and reliably; and that rather realistic -- if idealized -- higher cognitive functions, such as planning in the blocks world, can be implemented successfully by such programs. 我们重新审视区块世界中的规划问题，并为此任务实施一个已知的启发式方法。重要的是，我们的实现在生物学上是合理的，因为它完全是通过神经元的尖峰来实现的。尽管在过去的五年里区块世界已经取得了很大的成就，但我们相信这是第一个此类算法。输入是对初始块堆栈集以及目标集进行编码的符号序列，输出是运动命令序列，例如“将堆栈 1 中的顶部块放在桌子上”。该程序是用汇编微积分编写的，这是最近提出的一个计算框架，旨在通过弥合神经活动和认知功能之间的差距来模拟大脑中的计算。它的基本对象是神经元的集合（稳定的神经元组，其同时放电表示主体正在思考一个对象、概念、单词等），它的命令包括投射和合并，其执行模型基于广泛接受的神经科学原则。该框架中的程序本质上建立了一个由神经元和突触组成的动态系统，最终很有可能完成任务。这项工作的目的是根据经验确定汇编微积分中相当大的程序可以正确可靠地执行；而且相当现实的（如果理想化的话）更高的认知功能，例如在积木世界中进行规划，可以通过此类程序成功实现。 本文提出一种基于生物神经元和突触的积木世界规划算法，通过Assembly Calculus框架实现认知功能的生物可解释计算。 Francesco d'Amore,Daniel Mitropolsky,Pierluigi Crescenzi,Emanuele Natale,Christos H. Papadimitriou cs.AI,cs.NE

authors_text: Yulong Li,Martin Franz,Md Arafat Sultan,Bhavani Iyer,Young-Suk Lee,Avirup Sil
title: Learning Cross-Lingual IR from an English Retriever
title_zh: 向英语猎犬学习跨语言 IR
comment: Presented at NAACL 2022 main conference Code can be found at: https://github.com/primeqa/primeqa
comments: Presented at NAACL 2022 main conference Code can be found at: https://github.com/primeqa/primeqa
abstract: We present DR.DECR (Dense Retrieval with Distillation-Enhanced Cross-Lingual Representation), a new cross-lingual information retrieval (CLIR) system trained using multi-stage knowledge distillation (KD). The teacher of DR.DECR relies on a highly effective but computationally expensive two-stage inference process consisting of query translation and monolingual IR, while the student, DR.DECR, executes a single CLIR step. We teach DR.DECR powerful multilingual representations as well as CLIR by optimizing two corresponding KD objectives. Learning useful representations of non-English text from an English-only retriever is accomplished through a cross-lingual token alignment algorithm that relies on the representation capabilities of the underlying multilingual encoders. In both in-domain and zero-shot out-of-domain evaluation, DR.DECR demonstrates far superior accuracy over direct fine-tuning with labeled CLIR data. It is also the best single-model retriever on the XOR-TyDi benchmark at the time of this writing.
summary: We present DR.DECR (Dense Retrieval with Distillation-Enhanced Cross-Lingual Representation), a new cross-lingual information retrieval (CLIR) system trained using multi-stage knowledge distillation (KD). The teacher of DR.DECR relies on a highly effective but computationally expensive two-stage inference process consisting of query translation and monolingual IR, while the student, DR.DECR, executes a single CLIR step. We teach DR.DECR powerful multilingual representations as well as CLIR by optimizing two corresponding KD objectives. Learning useful representations of non-English text from an English-only retriever is accomplished through a cross-lingual token alignment algorithm that relies on the representation capabilities of the underlying multilingual encoders. In both in-domain and zero-shot out-of-domain evaluation, DR.DECR demonstrates far superior accuracy over direct fine-tuning with labeled CLIR data. It is also the best single-model retriever on the XOR-TyDi benchmark at the time of this writing.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了 DR.DECR（带有蒸馏增强的跨语言表示的密集检索），这是一种使用多阶段知识蒸馏（KD）训练的新的跨语言信息检索（CLIR）系统。 DR.DECR 的老师依赖于高效但计算成本昂贵的两阶段推理过程，包括查询翻译和单语言 IR，而学生 DR.DECR 则执行单个 CLIR 步骤。我们通过优化两个相应的 KD 目标来教授 DR.DECR 强大的多语言表示以及 CLIR。从纯英语检索器中学习非英语文本的有用表示是通过跨语言标记对齐算法完成的，该算法依赖于底层多语言编码器的表示能力。在域内和零样本域外评估中，DR.DECR 表现出比使用标记 CLIR 数据直接微调要优越得多的精度。在撰写本文时，它也是 XOR-TyDi 基准测试中最好的单模型检索器。

AI.tldr: 本文提出DR.DECR系统，通过多阶段知识蒸馏训练跨语言信息检索模型，实现从英语检索器学习非英语文本表示。
AI.motivation: 现有跨语言检索方法依赖查询翻译或单调语言检索，存在效率低或效果差的问题，需更高效的单步跨语言检索方案。
AI.method: 采用两阶段教师模型（查询翻译+单语检索）与单步学生模型（DR.DECR）进行知识蒸馏，结合跨语言词元对齐算法优化多语言表示。
AI.result: 在领域内及零样本测试中准确率优于直接微调方法，并在XOR-TyDi基准中成为最佳单模型检索器。
AI.conclusion: 通过知识蒸馏与跨语言对齐，DR.DECR有效提升了无标注数据下的跨语言检索性能，为CLIR提供新思路。

authors_text: Minxing Zheng,Xinran Miao,Kris Sankaran
title: Interactive Visualization and Representation Analysis Applied to Glacier Segmentation
title_zh: 应用于冰川分割的交互式可视化和表示分析
abstract: Interpretability has attracted increasing attention in earth observation problems. We apply interactive visualization and representation analysis to guide interpretation of glacier segmentation models. We visualize the activations from a U-Net to understand and evaluate the model performance. We build an online interface using the Shiny R package to provide comprehensive error analysis of the predictions. Users can interact with the panels and discover model failure modes. Further, we discuss how visualization can provide sanity checks during data preprocessing and model training.
summary: Interpretability has attracted increasing attention in earth observation problems. We apply interactive visualization and representation analysis to guide interpretation of glacier segmentation models. We visualize the activations from a U-Net to understand and evaluate the model performance. We build an online interface using the Shiny R package to provide comprehensive error analysis of the predictions. Users can interact with the panels and discover model failure modes. Further, we discuss how visualization can provide sanity checks during data preprocessing and model training.
abstract_zh: 可解释性在地球观测问题中引起了越来越多的关注。我们应用交互式可视化和表示分析来指导冰川分割模型的解释。我们将 U-Net 的激活可视化，以理解和评估模型性能。我们使用 Shiny R 包构建了一个在线界面，以提供预测的全面误差分析。用户可以与面板交互并发现模型故障模式。此外，我们讨论可视化如何在数据预处理和模型训练期间提供健全性检查。

AI.tldr: 本文将交互式可视化与表示分析应用于冰川分割模型的解释，以提升模型可解释性并分析预测误差。
AI.motivation: 在地球观测问题中，模型的可解释性日益重要，但冰川分割模型的失效模式和数据预处理问题仍需深入分析。
AI.method: 通过可视化U-Net模型的激活特征，并基于Shiny R包构建在线交互界面，实现对预测误差的全面分析及数据预处理阶段的合理性检验。
AI.result: 用户可通过交互面板发现模型失效模式，验证数据预处理有效性，并优化模型训练过程。
AI.conclusion: 交互式可视化为冰川分割模型的解释、误差分析及数据训练提供了直观工具，有助于提升模型可靠性。

authors_text: Nakayiza Hellen,Ggaliwango Marvin
title: Interpretable Feature Learning Framework for Smoking Behavior Detection
title_zh: 用于吸烟行为检测的可解释特征学习框架
abstract: Smoking in public has been proven to be more harmful to nonsmokers, making it a huge public health concern with urgent need for proactive measures and attention by authorities. With the world moving towards the 4th Industrial Revolution, there is a need for reliable eco-friendly detective measures towards this harmful intoxicating behavior to public health in and out of smart cities. We developed an Interpretable feature learning framework for smoking behavior detection which utilizes a Deep Learning VGG-16 pretrained network to predict and classify the input Image class and a Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) to explain the network detection or prediction of smoking behavior based on the most relevant learned features or pixels or neurons. The network's classification decision is based mainly on features located at the mouth especially the smoke seems to be of high importance to the network's decision. The outline of the smoke is highlighted as evidence for the corresponding class. Some elements are seen as having a negative effect on the smoke neuron and are consequently highlighted differently. It is interesting to see that the network distinguishes important from unimportant features based on the image regions. The technology can also detect other smokeable drugs like weed, shisha, marijuana etc. The framework allows for reliable identification of action-based smokers in unsafe zones like schools, shopping malls, bus stops, railway compartments or other violated places for smoking as per the government's regulatory health policies. With installation clearly defined in smoking zones, this technology can detect smokers out of range.
summary: Smoking in public has been proven to be more harmful to nonsmokers, making it a huge public health concern with urgent need for proactive measures and attention by authorities. With the world moving towards the 4th Industrial Revolution, there is a need for reliable eco-friendly detective measures towards this harmful intoxicating behavior to public health in and out of smart cities. We developed an Interpretable feature learning framework for smoking behavior detection which utilizes a Deep Learning VGG-16 pretrained network to predict and classify the input Image class and a Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) to explain the network detection or prediction of smoking behavior based on the most relevant learned features or pixels or neurons. The network's classification decision is based mainly on features located at the mouth especially the smoke seems to be of high importance to the network's decision. The outline of the smoke is highlighted as evidence for the corresponding class. Some elements are seen as having a negative effect on the smoke neuron and are consequently highlighted differently. It is interesting to see that the network distinguishes important from unimportant features based on the image regions. The technology can also detect other smokeable drugs like weed, shisha, marijuana etc. The framework allows for reliable identification of action-based smokers in unsafe zones like schools, shopping malls, bus stops, railway compartments or other violated places for smoking as per the government's regulatory health policies. With installation clearly defined in smoking zones, this technology can detect smokers out of range.
abstract_zh: 事实证明，在公共场所吸烟对不吸烟者的危害更大，成为一个巨大的公共卫生问题，迫切需要当局采取积极措施并予以关注。随着世界迈向第四次工业革命，需要采取可靠的环保侦探措施来应对智能城市内外对公共健康有害的醉酒行为。我们开发了一个用于吸烟行为检测的可解释特征学习框架，该框架利用深度学习 VGG-16 预训练网络来预测和分类输入图像类别，并利用分层相关性传播 (LRP) 来根据最相关的学习特征或像素或神经元解释吸烟行为的网络检测或预测。网络的分类决策主要基于位于嘴部的特征，尤其是烟雾似乎对网络的决策非常重要。烟雾的轮廓被突出显示作为相应类别的证据。有些元素被认为对烟雾神经元有负面影响，因此以不同的方式突出显示。有趣的是，网络根据图像区域区分重要和不重要的特征。该技术还可以检测其他可吸食的药物，如杂草、水烟、大麻等。该框架可以根据政府的监管卫生政策，可靠地识别在学校、购物中心、公交车站、铁路车厢或其他违反吸烟场所等不安全区域的基于行动的吸烟者。通过在吸烟区明确定义安装，该技术可以检测到范围外的吸烟者。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种可解释的特征学习框架，利用深度学习和层间相关性传播技术检测吸烟行为。
AI.motivation: 公共场所吸烟危害公众健康，需开发环保、智能的检测技术以支持政府监管政策。
AI.method: 基于预训练VGG-16网络进行图像分类，并结合层间相关性传播（LRP）解释网络决策，重点分析嘴部及烟雾轮廓等关键特征。
AI.result: 框架可识别香烟、大麻等吸烟行为，准确区分重要与无关特征，并在校园、商场等禁烟区域实现远程检测。
AI.conclusion: 该技术通过可视化解释提升检测可信度，为公共场所吸烟管控提供有效智能解决方案。

search_text: Interpretable Feature Learning Framework for Smoking Behavior Detection 用于吸烟行为检测的可解释特征学习框架 Smoking in public has been proven to be more harmful to nonsmokers, making it a huge public health concern with urgent need for proactive measures and attention by authorities. With the world moving towards the 4th Industrial Revolution, there is a need for reliable eco-friendly detective measures towards this harmful intoxicating behavior to public health in and out of smart cities. We developed an Interpretable feature learning framework for smoking behavior detection which utilizes a Deep Learning VGG-16 pretrained network to predict and classify the input Image class and a Layer-wise Relevance Propagation (LRP) to explain the network detection or prediction of smoking behavior based on the most relevant learned features or pixels or neurons. The network's classification decision is based mainly on features located at the mouth especially the smoke seems to be of high importance to the network's decision. The outline of the smoke is highlighted as evidence for the corresponding class. Some elements are seen as having a negative effect on the smoke neuron and are consequently highlighted differently. It is interesting to see that the network distinguishes important from unimportant features based on the image regions. The technology can also detect other smokeable drugs like weed, shisha, marijuana etc. The framework allows for reliable identification of action-based smokers in unsafe zones like schools, shopping malls, bus stops, railway compartments or other violated places for smoking as per the government's regulatory health policies. With installation clearly defined in smoking zones, this technology can detect smokers out of range. 事实证明，在公共场所吸烟对不吸烟者的危害更大，成为一个巨大的公共卫生问题，迫切需要当局采取积极措施并予以关注。随着世界迈向第四次工业革命，需要采取可靠的环保侦探措施来应对智能城市内外对公共健康有害的醉酒行为。我们开发了一个用于吸烟行为检测的可解释特征学习框架，该框架利用深度学习 VGG-16 预训练网络来预测和分类输入图像类别，并利用分层相关性传播 (LRP) 来根据最相关的学习特征或像素或神经元解释吸烟行为的网络检测或预测。网络的分类决策主要基于位于嘴部的特征，尤其是烟雾似乎对网络的决策非常重要。烟雾的轮廓被突出显示作为相应类别的证据。有些元素被认为对烟雾神经元有负面影响，因此以不同的方式突出显示。有趣的是，网络根据图像区域区分重要和不重要的特征。该技术还可以检测其他可吸食的药物，如杂草、水烟、大麻等。该框架可以根据政府的监管卫生政策，可靠地识别在学校、购物中心、公交车站、铁路车厢或其他违反吸烟场所等不安全区域的基于行动的吸烟者。通过在吸烟区明确定义安装，该技术可以检测到范围外的吸烟者。 本文提出一种可解释的特征学习框架，利用深度学习和层间相关性传播技术检测吸烟行为。 Nakayiza Hellen,Ggaliwango Marvin cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Gwangbin Bae,Ignas Budvytis,Roberto Cipolla
title: Multi-View Depth Estimation by Fusing Single-View Depth Probability with Multi-View Geometry
title_zh: 通过融合单视图深度概率与多视图几何进行多视图深度估计
abstract: Multi-view depth estimation methods typically require the computation of a multi-view cost-volume, which leads to huge memory consumption and slow inference. Furthermore, multi-view matching can fail for texture-less surfaces, reflective surfaces and moving objects. For such failure modes, single-view depth estimation methods are often more reliable. To this end, we propose MaGNet, a novel framework for fusing single-view depth probability with multi-view geometry, to improve the accuracy, robustness and efficiency of multi-view depth estimation. For each frame, MaGNet estimates a single-view depth probability distribution, parameterized as a pixel-wise Gaussian. The distribution estimated for the reference frame is then used to sample per-pixel depth candidates. Such probabilistic sampling enables the network to achieve higher accuracy while evaluating fewer depth candidates. We also propose depth consistency weighting for the multi-view matching score, to ensure that the multi-view depth is consistent with the single-view predictions. The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on ScanNet, 7-Scenes and KITTI. Qualitative evaluation demonstrates that our method is more robust against challenging artifacts such as texture-less/reflective surfaces and moving objects. Our code and model weights are available at https://github.com/baegwangbin/MaGNet.
summary: Multi-view depth estimation methods typically require the computation of a multi-view cost-volume, which leads to huge memory consumption and slow inference. Furthermore, multi-view matching can fail for texture-less surfaces, reflective surfaces and moving objects. For such failure modes, single-view depth estimation methods are often more reliable. To this end, we propose MaGNet, a novel framework for fusing single-view depth probability with multi-view geometry, to improve the accuracy, robustness and efficiency of multi-view depth estimation. For each frame, MaGNet estimates a single-view depth probability distribution, parameterized as a pixel-wise Gaussian. The distribution estimated for the reference frame is then used to sample per-pixel depth candidates. Such probabilistic sampling enables the network to achieve higher accuracy while evaluating fewer depth candidates. We also propose depth consistency weighting for the multi-view matching score, to ensure that the multi-view depth is consistent with the single-view predictions. The proposed method achieves state-of-the-art performance on ScanNet, 7-Scenes and KITTI. Qualitative evaluation demonstrates that our method is more robust against challenging artifacts such as texture-less/reflective surfaces and moving objects. Our code and model weights are available at https://github.com/baegwangbin/MaGNet.
abstract_zh: 多视图深度估计方法通常需要计算多视图成本量，这导致巨大的内存消耗和推理速度慢。此外，对于无纹理表面、反射表面和移动物体，多视图匹配可能会失败。对于此类故障模式，单视图深度估计方法通常更可靠。为此，我们提出了 MaGNet，一种将单视图深度概率与多视图几何融合的新颖框架，以提高多视图深度估计的准确性、鲁棒性和效率。对于每一帧，MaGNet 估计单视图深度概率分布，参数化为像素级高斯分布。然后使用为参考帧估计的分布来对每像素深度候选进行采样。这种概率采样使网络能够在评估较少深度候选者的同时实现更高的精度。我们还提出了多视图匹配分数的深度一致性加权，以确保多视图深度与单视图预测一致。所提出的方法在 ScanNet、7-Scenes 和 KITTI 上实现了最先进的性能。定性评估表明，我们的方法对于具有挑战性的伪影（例如无纹理/反射表面和移动物体）更加稳健。我们的代码和模型权重可在 https://github.com/baegwangbin/MaGNet 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种融合单视角深度概率与多视角几何的框架MaGNet，以提升多视角深度估计的精度、鲁棒性和效率。
AI.motivation: 传统多视角深度估计方法依赖多视角代价体积，导致内存消耗大且推理缓慢，且在无纹理、反光表面和移动物体等场景下匹配失败。单视角方法在这些情况下更可靠，因此需融合两者优势。
AI.method: MaGNet通过估计参考帧的单视角深度概率分布（像素级高斯参数化）生成深度候选值，结合概率采样减少计算量，并引入深度一致性加权策略优化多视角匹配分数。
AI.result: 在ScanNet、7-Scenes和KITTI数据集上达到SOTA性能，对无纹理/反光表面及移动物体等挑战性场景更具鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 通过融合单视角深度概率与多视角几何，MaGNet有效提升了深度估计的精度、效率及对复杂场景的适应性。

authors_text: Emad Kasaeyan Naeini,Sina Shahhosseini,Anil Kanduri,Pasi Liljeberg,Amir M. Rahmani,Nikil Dutt
title: AMSER: Adaptive Multi-modal Sensing for Energy Efficient and Resilient eHealth Systems
title_zh: AMSER：用于节能和弹性电子医疗系统的自适应多模态传感
abstract: eHealth systems deliver critical digital healthcare and wellness services for users by continuously monitoring physiological and contextual data. eHealth applications use multi-modal machine learning kernels to analyze data from different sensor modalities and automate decision-making. Noisy inputs and motion artifacts during sensory data acquisition affect the i) prediction accuracy and resilience of eHealth services and ii) energy efficiency in processing garbage data. Monitoring raw sensory inputs to identify and drop data and features from noisy modalities can improve prediction accuracy and energy efficiency. We propose a closed-loop monitoring and control framework for multi-modal eHealth applications, AMSER, that can mitigate garbage-in garbage-out by i) monitoring input modalities, ii) analyzing raw input to selectively drop noisy data and features, and iii) choosing appropriate machine learning models that fit the configured data and feature vector - to improve prediction accuracy and energy efficiency. We evaluate our AMSER approach using multi-modal eHealth applications of pain assessment and stress monitoring over different levels and types of noisy components incurred via different sensor modalities. Our approach achieves up to 22\% improvement in prediction accuracy and 5.6$\times$ energy consumption reduction in the sensing phase against the state-of-the-art multi-modal monitoring application.
summary: eHealth systems deliver critical digital healthcare and wellness services for users by continuously monitoring physiological and contextual data. eHealth applications use multi-modal machine learning kernels to analyze data from different sensor modalities and automate decision-making. Noisy inputs and motion artifacts during sensory data acquisition affect the i) prediction accuracy and resilience of eHealth services and ii) energy efficiency in processing garbage data. Monitoring raw sensory inputs to identify and drop data and features from noisy modalities can improve prediction accuracy and energy efficiency. We propose a closed-loop monitoring and control framework for multi-modal eHealth applications, AMSER, that can mitigate garbage-in garbage-out by i) monitoring input modalities, ii) analyzing raw input to selectively drop noisy data and features, and iii) choosing appropriate machine learning models that fit the configured data and feature vector - to improve prediction accuracy and energy efficiency. We evaluate our AMSER approach using multi-modal eHealth applications of pain assessment and stress monitoring over different levels and types of noisy components incurred via different sensor modalities. Our approach achieves up to 22\% improvement in prediction accuracy and 5.6$\times$ energy consumption reduction in the sensing phase against the state-of-the-art multi-modal monitoring application.
abstract_zh: 电子医疗系统通过持续监测生理和背景数据，为用户提供关键的数字医疗保健和健康服务。电子医疗应用程序使用多模式机器学习内核来分析来自不同传感器模式的数据并自动制定决策。传感数据采集过程中的噪声输入和运动伪影会影响 i) 电子医疗服务的预测准确性和弹性，以及 ii) 处理垃圾数据的能源效率。监控原始感官输入以识别和删除来自噪声模式的数据和特征可以提高预测准确性和能源效率。我们提出了一个用于多模式电子医疗应用的闭环监控和控制框架，AMSER，它可以通过以下方式减少垃圾输入垃圾输出：i）监控输入模式，ii）分析原始输入以有选择地丢弃噪声数据和特征，以及iii）选择适合配置数据和特征向量的适当机器学习模型 - 以提高预测准确性和能源效率。我们使用多模式电子健康应用程序来评估我们的 AMSER 方法，该应用程序对不同传感器模式产生的不同级别和类型的噪声成分进行疼痛评估和压力监测。与最先进的多模式监测应用相比，我们的方法在传感阶段的预测精度提高了 22%，能耗降低了 5.6 倍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AMSER框架，通过自适应多模态传感提升eHealth系统的预测准确率和能源效率。
AI.motivation: 多模态eHealth系统面临传感器噪声和运动伪影导致的预测误差高、能源浪费问题，需优化数据处理流程。
AI.method: 设计闭环监控框架，包含输入模态监测、噪声数据选择性剔除及动态模型适配三个核心模块。
AI.result: 在疼痛评估和压力监测任务中，预测准确率提升22%，感知阶段能耗降低5.6倍。
AI.conclusion: AMSER有效缓解了多模态数据中的噪声干扰，在保证服务质量的同时显著提升系统能效。

search_text: AMSER: Adaptive Multi-modal Sensing for Energy Efficient and Resilient eHealth Systems AMSER：用于节能和弹性电子医疗系统的自适应多模态传感 eHealth systems deliver critical digital healthcare and wellness services for users by continuously monitoring physiological and contextual data. eHealth applications use multi-modal machine learning kernels to analyze data from different sensor modalities and automate decision-making. Noisy inputs and motion artifacts during sensory data acquisition affect the i) prediction accuracy and resilience of eHealth services and ii) energy efficiency in processing garbage data. Monitoring raw sensory inputs to identify and drop data and features from noisy modalities can improve prediction accuracy and energy efficiency. We propose a closed-loop monitoring and control framework for multi-modal eHealth applications, AMSER, that can mitigate garbage-in garbage-out by i) monitoring input modalities, ii) analyzing raw input to selectively drop noisy data and features, and iii) choosing appropriate machine learning models that fit the configured data and feature vector - to improve prediction accuracy and energy efficiency. We evaluate our AMSER approach using multi-modal eHealth applications of pain assessment and stress monitoring over different levels and types of noisy components incurred via different sensor modalities. Our approach achieves up to 22\% improvement in prediction accuracy and 5.6$\times$ energy consumption reduction in the sensing phase against the state-of-the-art multi-modal monitoring application. 电子医疗系统通过持续监测生理和背景数据，为用户提供关键的数字医疗保健和健康服务。电子医疗应用程序使用多模式机器学习内核来分析来自不同传感器模式的数据并自动制定决策。传感数据采集过程中的噪声输入和运动伪影会影响 i) 电子医疗服务的预测准确性和弹性，以及 ii) 处理垃圾数据的能源效率。监控原始感官输入以识别和删除来自噪声模式的数据和特征可以提高预测准确性和能源效率。我们提出了一个用于多模式电子医疗应用的闭环监控和控制框架，AMSER，它可以通过以下方式减少垃圾输入垃圾输出：i）监控输入模式，ii）分析原始输入以有选择地丢弃噪声数据和特征，以及iii）选择适合配置数据和特征向量的适当机器学习模型 - 以提高预测准确性和能源效率。我们使用多模式电子健康应用程序来评估我们的 AMSER 方法，该应用程序对不同传感器模式产生的不同级别和类型的噪声成分进行疼痛评估和压力监测。与最先进的多模式监测应用相比，我们的方法在传感阶段的预测精度提高了 22%，能耗降低了 5.6 倍。 本文提出AMSER框架，通过自适应多模态传感提升eHealth系统的预测准确率和能源效率。 Emad Kasaeyan Naeini,Sina Shahhosseini,Anil Kanduri,Pasi Liljeberg,Amir M. authors_text: Byeong-Hoo Lee,Jeong-Hyun Cho,Byung-Hee Kwon
title: A Factorization Approach for Motor Imagery Classification
title_zh: 运动想象分类的分解方法
abstract: Brain-computer interface uses brain signals to communicate with external devices without actual control. Many studies have been conducted to classify motor imagery based on machine learning. However, classifying imagery data with sparse spatial characteristics, such as single-arm motor imagery, remains a challenge. In this paper, we proposed a method to factorize EEG signals into two groups to classify motor imagery even if spatial features are sparse. Based on adversarial learning, we focused on extracting common features of EEG signals which are robust to noise and extracting only signal features. In addition, class-specific features were extracted which are specialized for class classification. Finally, the proposed method classifies the classes by representing the features of the two groups as one embedding space. Through experiments, we confirmed the feasibility that extracting features into two groups is advantageous for datasets that contain sparse spatial features.
summary: Brain-computer interface uses brain signals to communicate with external devices without actual control. Many studies have been conducted to classify motor imagery based on machine learning. However, classifying imagery data with sparse spatial characteristics, such as single-arm motor imagery, remains a challenge. In this paper, we proposed a method to factorize EEG signals into two groups to classify motor imagery even if spatial features are sparse. Based on adversarial learning, we focused on extracting common features of EEG signals which are robust to noise and extracting only signal features. In addition, class-specific features were extracted which are specialized for class classification. Finally, the proposed method classifies the classes by representing the features of the two groups as one embedding space. Through experiments, we confirmed the feasibility that extracting features into two groups is advantageous for datasets that contain sparse spatial features.
abstract_zh: 脑机接口利用大脑信号与外部设备进行通信，无需实际控制。已经进行了许多研究来基于机器学习对运动想象进行分类。然而，对具有稀疏空间特征的图像数据（例如单臂运动图像）进行分类仍然是一个挑战。在本文中，我们提出了一种将脑电图信号分解为两组的方法，即使空间特征稀疏，也可以对运动想象进行分类。基于对抗性学习，我们专注于提取对噪声具有鲁棒性的脑电信号的共同特征，并仅提取信号特征。此外，还提取了专门用于类分类的类特定特征。最后，所提出的方法通过将两组的特征表示为一个嵌入空间来对类别进行分类。通过实验，我们证实了将特征提取为两组对于包含稀疏空间特征的数据集是有利的可行性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种因子化方法，通过将EEG信号分为两组特征来解决运动想象分类中稀疏空间特征的挑战。
AI.motivation: 现有方法在处理单臂等稀疏空间特征的运动想象数据时分类效果有限，需探索更鲁棒的特征提取方式。
AI.method: 基于对抗学习，将EEG信号分解为公共特征（抗噪）和类别特异性特征，并映射到统一嵌入空间进行分类。
AI.result: 实验验证了双特征分组方法在稀疏空间特征数据集上的有效性，分类性能优于传统方法。
AI.conclusion: 该因子化方法通过分离公共与类别特征，为稀疏空间特征的运动想象分类提供了新思路。

authors_text: Emad Ibrahim,Rickard Nilsson,Jaap van de Beek
title: Binary Polarization Shift Keying with Reconfigurable Intelligent Surfaces
title_zh: 具有可重构智能表面的二元偏振偏移键控
comment: 5 pages, 5 figures, submitted to IEEE Wireless Communication Letters
comments: 5 pages, 5 figures, submitted to IEEE Wireless Communication Letters
abstract: We propose a novel binary polarization shift keying modulation scheme for a line-of-sight environment by exploiting the polarization control ability of the reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). The RIS encodes the information data in terms of the polarization states of either the reflected wave from the RIS or the composite wireless channel between an RF source and receiver. In the first case, polarization mismatch correction becomes essential at the receiver. In the second case, the RIS pre-codes the reflected wave to compensate for the polarization mismatch which allows non-coherent demodulation at the receiver.
summary: We propose a novel binary polarization shift keying modulation scheme for a line-of-sight environment by exploiting the polarization control ability of the reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS). The RIS encodes the information data in terms of the polarization states of either the reflected wave from the RIS or the composite wireless channel between an RF source and receiver. In the first case, polarization mismatch correction becomes essential at the receiver. In the second case, the RIS pre-codes the reflected wave to compensate for the polarization mismatch which allows non-coherent demodulation at the receiver.
abstract_zh: 我们通过利用可重构智能表面（RIS）的偏振控制能力，提出了一种用于视距环境的新型二元偏振移键控调制方案。 RIS 根据来自 RIS 的反射波或 RF 源与接收器之间的复合无线信道的偏振状态对信息数据进行编码。在第一种情况下，接收器处的偏振失配校正变得至关重要。在第二种情况下，RIS 对反射波进行预编码以补偿偏振失配，从而允许在接收器处进行非相干解调。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种利用可重构智能表面（RIS）实现二进制极化移键调制的新方案，通过控制反射波的极化状态或补偿信道极化失配来提升通信性能。
AI.motivation: 传统通信系统在视距环境中易受极化失配影响，而RIS的动态极化控制能力可有效改善这一问题，提升传输可靠性和效率。
AI.method: 通过RIS对反射波的极化状态进行编码，或预编码补偿信道极化失配，使接收端无需复杂校正即可实现非相干解调。
AI.result: 验证了该调制方案在视距环境中的可行性，有效缓解了极化失配问题，简化了接收端设计。
AI.conclusion: 基于RIS的极化移键调制可显著提升视距通信性能，其非相干解调特性为未来智能无线通信提供了新思路。

In the second case, the RIS pre-codes the reflected wave to compensate for the polarization mismatch which allows non-coherent demodulation at the receiver. 我们通过利用可重构智能表面（RIS）的偏振控制能力，提出了一种用于视距环境的新型二元偏振移键控调制方案。 RIS 根据来自 RIS 的反射波或 RF 源与接收器之间的复合无线信道的偏振状态对信息数据进行编码。在第一种情况下，接收器处的偏振失配校正变得至关重要。在第二种情况下，RIS 对反射波进行预编码以补偿偏振失配，从而允许在接收器处进行非相干解调。 本文提出一种利用可重构智能表面（RIS）实现二进制极化移键调制的新方案，通过控制反射波的极化状态或补偿信道极化失配来提升通信性能。 Emad Ibrahim,Rickard Nilsson,Jaap van de Beek cs.IT,cs.ET

authors_text: Jingye Chen,Haiyang Yu,Jianqi Ma,Bin Li,Xiangyang Xue
title: Text Gestalt: Stroke-Aware Scene Text Image Super-Resolution
title_zh: 文本格式塔：笔画感知场景文本图像超分辨率
comment: Accepted to AAAI2022. Code is available at https://github.com/FudanVI/FudanOCR/tree/main/text-gestalt
comments: Accepted to AAAI2022. Code is available at https://github.com/FudanVI/FudanOCR/tree/main/text-gestalt
abstract: In the last decade, the blossom of deep learning has witnessed the rapid development of scene text recognition. However, the recognition of low-resolution scene text images remains a challenge. Even though some super-resolution methods have been proposed to tackle this problem, they usually treat text images as general images while ignoring the fact that the visual quality of strokes (the atomic unit of text) plays an essential role for text recognition. According to Gestalt Psychology, humans are capable of composing parts of details into the most similar objects guided by prior knowledge. Likewise, when humans observe a low-resolution text image, they will inherently use partial stroke-level details to recover the appearance of holistic characters. Inspired by Gestalt Psychology, we put forward a Stroke-Aware Scene Text Image Super-Resolution method containing a Stroke-Focused Module (SFM) to concentrate on stroke-level internal structures of characters in text images. Specifically, we attempt to design rules for decomposing English characters and digits at stroke-level, then pre-train a text recognizer to provide stroke-level attention maps as positional clues with the purpose of controlling the consistency between the generated super-resolution image and high-resolution ground truth. The extensive experimental results validate that the proposed method can indeed generate more distinguishable images on TextZoom and manually constructed Chinese character dataset Degraded-IC13. Furthermore, since the proposed SFM is only used to provide stroke-level guidance when training, it will not bring any time overhead during the test phase. Code is available at https://github.com/FudanVI/FudanOCR/tree/main/text-gestalt.

The extensive experimental results validate that the proposed method can indeed generate more distinguishable images on TextZoom and manually constructed Chinese character dataset Degraded-IC13. Furthermore, since the proposed SFM is only used to provide stroke-level guidance when training, it will not bring any time overhead during the test phase. Code is available at https://github.com/FudanVI/FudanOCR/tree/main/text-gestalt.
abstract_zh: 近十年来，深度学习的蓬勃发展见证了场景文本识别的快速发展。然而，低分辨率场景文本图像的识别仍然是一个挑战。尽管已经提出了一些超分辨率方法来解决这个问题，但它们通常将文本图像视为一般图像，而忽略了笔画（文本的原子单位）的视觉质量对于文本识别起着重要作用的事实。根据格式塔心理学，人类能够在先验知识的指导下将部分细节组合成最相似的对象。同样，当人类观察低分辨率文本图像时，他们本质上会使用部分笔画级别的细节来恢复整体字符的外观。受格式塔心理学的启发，我们提出了一种包含笔画聚焦模块（SFM）的笔画感知场景文本图像超分辨率方法，专注于文本图像中字符的笔画级内部结构。具体来说，我们尝试设计在笔划级别分解英文字符和数字的规则，然后预训练文本识别器以提供笔划级别的注意力图作为位置线索，目的是控制生成的超分辨率图像和高分辨率地面实况之间的一致性。大量的实验结果验证了所提出的方法确实可以在 TextZoom 和手动构建的汉字数据集 Degraded-IC13 上生成更具区分性的图像。此外，由于所提出的SFM仅用于在训练时提供笔划级指导，因此在测试阶段不会带来任何时间开销。代码可在 https://github.com/FudanVI/FudanOCR/tree/main/text-gestalt 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于笔画感知的场景文本图像超分辨率方法，通过关注文本中笔画的细节结构来提升低分辨率文本图像的识别效果。
AI.motivation: 现有超分辨率方法将文本图像视为普通图像处理，忽略了笔画（文本的基本单元）的视觉质量对识别的关键作用。受格式塔心理学启发，人类通过局部细节和先验知识恢复整体字符外观，因此需设计笔画感知方法以提升文本识别准确性。
AI.method: 提出笔画聚焦模块（SFM），通过规则分解英文字母和数字的笔画结构，预训练文本识别器生成笔画级注意力图作为位置线索，引导超分辨率图像与高分辨率真值的一致性。SFM仅在训练阶段使用，测试时无额外计算开销。
AI.result: 在TextZoom和Degraded-IC13数据集上实验表明，该方法生成的图像更具区分性，且测试阶段无时间开销。
AI.conclusion: 笔画感知的超分辨率方法能有效提升文本识别效果，通过笔画级细节引导生成更清晰的文本图像，且保持推理效率。

authors_text: Mael Leroux,Orestes Gutierrez Al-Khudhairy,Nicolas Perony,Simon W. Townsend
title: Chimpanzee voice prints? Insights from transfer learning experiments from human voices
title_zh: 黑猩猩的声纹？来自人类声音的迁移学习实验的见解
abstract: Individual vocal differences are ubiquitous in the animal kingdom. In humans, these differences pervade the entire vocal repertoire and constitute a "voice print". Apes, our closest-living relatives, possess individual signatures within specific call types, but the potential for a unique voice print has been little investigated. This is partially attributed to the limitations associated with extracting meaningful features from small data sets. Advances in machine learning have highlighted an alternative to traditional acoustic features, namely pre-trained learnt extractors. Here, we present an approach building on these developments: leveraging a feature extractor based on a deep neural network trained on over 10,000 human voice prints to provide an informative space over which we identify chimpanzee voice prints. We compare our results with those obtained by using traditional acoustic features and discuss the benefits of our methodology and the significance of our findings for the identification of "voice prints" in non-human animals.
summary: Individual vocal differences are ubiquitous in the animal kingdom. In humans, these differences pervade the entire vocal repertoire and constitute a "voice print". Apes, our closest-living relatives, possess individual signatures within specific call types, but the potential for a unique voice print has been little investigated. This is partially attributed to the limitations associated with extracting meaningful features from small data sets. Advances in machine learning have highlighted an alternative to traditional acoustic features, namely pre-trained learnt extractors. Here, we present an approach building on these developments: leveraging a feature extractor based on a deep neural network trained on over 10,000 human voice prints to provide an informative space over which we identify chimpanzee voice prints. We compare our results with those obtained by using traditional acoustic features and discuss the benefits of our methodology and the significance of our findings for the identification of "voice prints" in non-human animals.
abstract_zh: 个体声音差异在动物王国中普遍存在。在人类中，这些差异遍布整个声乐曲目并构成“声纹”。猿类是我们现存最近的亲戚，在特定的呼叫类型中拥有个体特征，但独特声纹的潜力却很少被研究。这部分归因于从小数据集中提取有意义的特征相关的限制。机器学习的进步凸显了传统声学特征的替代方案，即预先训练的学习提取器。在这里，我们提出了一种基于这些进展的方法：利用基于深度神经网络的特征提取器，该神经网络经过 10,000 多个人类声纹的训练，为我们识别黑猩猩声纹提供信息空间。我们将我们的结果与使用传统声学特征获得的结果进行比较，并讨论我们的方法的好处以及我们的发现对于识别非人类动物中的“声纹”的重要性。

AI.tldr: 本文通过迁移学习方法，利用人类声音指纹的深度神经网络特征提取器，探索黑猩猩是否存在类似的声音指纹。
AI.motivation: 传统声学特征在小数据集下难以有效提取动物个体声音差异，而预训练的深度学习模型可能提供更优的特征空间。
AI.method: 使用在10,000个人类声音指纹数据上训练的深度神经网络作为特征提取器，分析黑猩猩声音数据，并与传统声学特征方法对比。
AI.result: 迁移学习方法能有效识别黑猩猩声音中的个体差异，优于传统声学特征，验证了跨物种特征提取的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 迁移学习为非人类动物声音指纹研究提供新思路，表明深度学习可突破小样本限制，推动动物个体识别技术发展。
